what_it_does: This file is a generated surface-spec blueprint for a planned mobile view named G2, located in the unrouted surfaces directory; it declares its identity, platform scope, build targets, and intended journey/cross-journey address references, all currently empty.
observations: The surface is for the 'mobile' platform only and is titled 'Mobile navigation model'
It is in status 'planned' and its home_journey is '_unrouted'
journeys, cross_journeys, addresses, represents, and build_to.components are all empty arrays
build_to specifies a design lint command and a token source path
It explicitly warns that addresses must not be invented and must be built to the listed addresses
standouts: Empty routing/address graph despite being described as carrying the web (B3)
Explicit anti-invention rule for addresses in the '_what' preamble
Status 'planned' with no components built yet
concerns: The file claims journeys/cross_journeys carry the web (B3) but contains none, so it cannot currently fulfill that role
Empty arrays make it hard to verify intended surface boundaries without external context
notes: A placeholder mobile surface blueprint that has declared its lint and token build targets but has not yet been wired to any journeys, addresses, or components.
questions: Which journeys and cross_journeys will eventually route through G2?
What does 'carry the web (B3)' mean for this surface?
What components are expected in build_to.components once the surface is implemented?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to be a generated surface-spec with journey address refs carrying the web (B3); actual content is an unrouted, empty shell, so it does not yet match that purpose.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to establish a machine-readable surface definition that will later be populated with journey references and components, with build tooling driven by lint and token sources.
novelty: The '_what' preamble acts as a self-documenting provenance note that forbids invented addresses, which is unusual for a generated spec file.
contribution: Provides a planned mobile surface slot with explicit build-to hooks (lint command and token source) and a clear rule that addresses must be real, which a fused system could keep as a scaffold for future routing.
summary_for_embedding: A planned, unrouted mobile surface blueprint G2 with empty journey and component references but defined build-to lint and token sources.
intention_for_embedding: To reserve a generated, address-bound mobile surface specification for later journey wiring and component assignment.
